idea-plugin.name: igni
idea-plugin.description:
igni AI coding assistant — chat tool window backed by a managed local Ember backend.

Nothing to install: the plugin downloads uv on first launch and uses it to provision Python and ignite-ember into a per-user cache. Network access is required on first run only.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.6.4

  • Auto-updater finally works on macOS. Required two missing pieces: bundle.createUpdaterArtifacts: true in tauri.conf.json so the .app.tar.gz + .sig actually get emitted, and a workflow step that stashes the .sig before tauri-action's upload pass cleans it up. Includes a minisign re-sign fallback if the .sig is gone by the time we reach for it. v0.6.0 / 0.6.1 / 0.6.2 / 0.6.3 clients will get a real update prompt for 0.6.4 — the first release with a valid latest.json attached.
  • Same product surface as 0.6.1 — no UI changes.

0.6.3

  • Re-attempt of the 0.6.2 updater fix. The previous workflow used actions/upload-artifact's glob to stash .app.tar.gz.sig from the macOS build dirs, but the glob silently matched zero files even though the file was at exactly that path, failing both macOS matrix legs. Switched to a find + cp step that copies the sig to a stable no-spaces path before the upload-artifact step picks it up.
  • Same set of features as 0.6.1 — frosted-glass header / sidebar, custom scroll indicators, faster post-stream tail, knowledge-index warmup, window-drag from the chat header, dark-tile app icon, generic DMG installer icon.

0.6.2

  • Release-pipeline fix: latest.json for the Tauri auto-updater is now assembled and uploaded by our own workflow step. tauri-action's built-in path was silently skipping with "Signature not found for the updater JSON" on Tauri 2 + product names with spaces, so previous releases shipped without the updater manifest and clients couldn't detect new versions. Users on 0.6.0 / 0.6.1 will receive an update prompt for 0.6.2.
  • No JetBrains-side changes in this release; bundled ignite-ember bumped to 0.6.2 in lockstep via build.gradle.kts.

0.6.1

  • Frosted-glass header + sidebar — messages scrolling behind the brand row and the "Sessions" cluster now fade through a six-layer progressive backdrop blur instead of being clipped at a hard edge. Custom scroll-position indicators paint above the blur so the thumb stays sharp.
  • Faster perceived response time — Agno's per-turn auto user-profile + session-context extractions are off by default. They were firing a separate LLM call after each run, adding 5–10 s between the visible answer ending and the stats line landing. Agentic memory tools still run on demand.
  • Knowledge index warms in the background at session start, so the first /knowledge press no longer waits on the Chroma client to open.
  • Window dragging from the chat header in the Tauri shell (was previously gated by a missing core:window:allow-start-dragging capability). New dark-tile app icon; downloaded .dmg no longer shows the app icon on the disk image.

0.6.0

  • Multi-agent orchestration — the chat panel now drives a session pool, so you can run multiple parallel conversations (one per project, or N per project) in the same IDE window. Each tool window gets its own session, with live typing + message mirroring across IDE / web / Tauri views bound to the same session.
  • Project-lock UX — header chip shows which project the session is bound to; backend-managed directory picker for switching between repos without losing context.
  • BE reliability pass — WAL-mode SQLite for the per- session stores, idle eviction so a long-lived BE releases memory once a session goes dormant, and a slow-client back-pressure fix so a single wedged view never stalls the other sessions' streams.
  • First-launch onboarding polish — clearer progress during the uv/Python bootstrap, IDE proxy is now propagated to the backend, disk-space precheck.

0.1.0

  • Zero-touch install — plugin bootstraps uv + Python + ignite-ember on first launch instead of requiring a manual pip install.
  • Editor integration — right-click selection / project view → "Add to Ember chat"; ⌘E to focus the chat panel.
  • Edits via the IDE — backend writes now route through WriteCommandAction so Local History records the change and editor undo works.
  • Indexed code search — paste handler uses PyCharm's trigram index for near-instant lookups instead of the rg subprocess.
  • IDE theme follows the chat panel (and vice-versa).

action.description: Send the current editor selection to the Ember chat composer
Attach this file (or selected files) to the Ember chat composer
Stop the Ember Python backend and start a fresh one
Wipe the managed Python cache and bootstrap from scratch
Dump plugin, CLI, and env information for bug reports
